tossups.question: A Maysles documentary describes a rancher's experience sleeping beside an artwork by this man. Cologne Harbor was the site of an early artwork by this man titled Dockside Packages. For decades, this man tried to gain permission from the United Arab Emirates to build the stacked-oil-barrel sculpture Mastaba. This man created several large, inflatable indoor installations known as Air Packages. He used saffron-colored fabric to construct 7,503 structures in Central Park in his project The Gates, which, like virtually all of his work, was performed in collaboration with his Moroccan-born French wife Jean-Claude. For 10 points, name this Bulgarian mononymic artist who is best known for his part in wrapping the Reichstag.
tossups.answer_sanitized: Christo Vladimirov Javacheff [or Christo Vladimirov Javacheff]
tossups.question_sanitized: A Maysles documentary describes a rancher's experience sleeping beside an artwork by this man. Cologne Harbor was the site of an early artwork by this man titled Dockside Packages. For decades, this man tried to gain permission from the United Arab Emirates to build the stacked-oil-barrel sculpture Mastaba. This man created several large, inflatable indoor installations known as Air Packages. He used saffron-colored fabric to construct 7,503 structures in Central Park in his project The Gates, which, like virtually all of his work, was performed in collaboration with his Moroccan-born French wife Jean-Claude. For 10 points, name this Bulgarian mononymic artist who is best known for his part in wrapping the Reichstag.

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about art in the films of the late David Lynch, for 10 points each.
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Answer the following about art in the films of the late David Lynch, for 10 points each.
parts: On Twin Peaks, the hallway of the "red room" contains this ancient Greek sculpture of a love goddess lacking both her arms.

Also on Twin Peaks, the imagery of Laura Palmer's body may have been inspired by this artist's early work "Wrapped Woman." This artist hung orange nylon from hundreds of gates in Central Park with his partner Jeanne-Claude.

Several Lynch sets were inspired by abandoned textile factories in this country, the implied setting of Inland Empire. A sculptor from this country used burlap to create headless sculptures, recreated in the installation Agora.
parts_sanitized: On Twin Peaks, the hallway of the "red room" contains this ancient Greek sculpture of a love goddess lacking both her arms.

Also on Twin Peaks, the imagery of Laura Palmer's body may have been inspired by this artist's early work "Wrapped Woman." This artist hung orange nylon from hundreds of gates in Central Park with his partner Jeanne-Claude.

Several Lynch sets were inspired by abandoned textile factories in this country, the implied setting of Inland Empire. A sculptor from this country used burlap to create headless sculptures, recreated in the installation Agora.
answers: Poland [or Republic of Poland or Rzeczpospolita Polska] (The unmentioned artist is Magdalena Abakanowicz.)
answers_sanitized: Venus de Milo [or Aphrodite of Melos]
Poland [or Republic of Poland or Rzeczpospolita Polska] (The unmentioned artist is Magdalena Abakanowicz.)

bonuses.leadin: For his Anthropometries series, Yves Klein painted nude models this color and had them make imprints on a blank canvas as “human paintbrushes.” For 10 points each:
parts: Name this color that Klein developed a namesake international shade of using ultramarine. Pigments of this primary color can be produced from lapis lazuli.

Klein created a headless plaster cast of this sculpture completely covered in blue. Alexander of Antioch is believed to have created this armless sculpture that features 28 times in Dalí’s The Hallucinogenic Toreador.
These two artists were both part of the Nouveau réalisme movement founded by Yves Klein. This husband-and-wife duo designed large landmark projects such as the Wrapped Reichstag.
answers: Christo and Jeanne-Claude [or Christo Vladimirov Javacheff and Jeanne-Claude Denat de Guillebon; prompt on partial answer]
answers_sanitized: Venus de Milo [or Aphrodite of Melos]
Christo and Jeanne-Claude [or Christo Vladimirov Javacheff and Jeanne-Claude Denat de Guillebon; prompt on partial answer]

bonuses.leadin: Sometimes transporting works of art can be a deadly business. For 10 points each:
parts: Two engineers were killed when the base collapsed on a 3-ton sculpture by this artist that was being installed in Princeton. One of this artist's signature mobiles hangs in the East Building of the National Gallery of Art.
A rigger was killed when a portion of this artist's Sculpture No. 3. fell. This man's controversial Tilted Arc was removed from Foley Federal Plaza in 1989.

This man's Umbrellas project was dismantled after one fatally broke loose; unfortunately, a Japanese worker was electrocuted in the dismantling. This man collaborated on The Gates in Central Park with JeanneClaude.

This man's Umbrellas project was dismantled after one fatally broke loose; unfortunately, a Japanese worker was electrocuted in the dismantling. This man collaborated on The Gates in Central Park with JeanneClaude.

bonuses.leadin: A 1981 photograph shows this man wrapped head to toe in fabric. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Bulgarian artist who died in May 2020 and is known for his many collaborations with his wife that include 1995’s Wrapped Reichstag.
This photographer took that picture of Christo. Meryl Streep tugs diagonally at the corners of her white painted face in a Rolling Stone cover photograph by this artist.

The aforementioned Leibovitz photograph was taken at this New York location where Jean-Claude and Christo created their installation The Gates. Diane Arbus photographed a child with a hand grenade in this Frederick Law Olmstead designed park.
answers_sanitized: Christo Vladimirov Javacheff [accept either underlined portion]

bonuses.leadin: For an exhibit running from June to October 2016, an artist installed one of these features outside the Palace of Versailles's Grand Canal. For 10 points each:
answers: Christo Vladimirov Javacheff AND Jeanne-Claude Denat de Guillebon [prompt on partial answers]
parts: In 2008, the Danish-Icelandic artist Olafur Eliasson ("oh-lah-foor el-EE-ah-son") recreated what sort of natural feature underneath Brooklyn Bridge?
In 2008, Eliasson ("el-EE-ah-son") surpassed this couple's installations of orange gates in Central Park as the most-expensive public arts project. This couple also wrapped the Reichstag in plastic.

In 2018, Eliasson ("el-EE-ah-son") installed blocks of ice in this city to showcase the negative effects of climate change. Eliasson ("el-EE-ah-son") also created The Weather Project for this city's Tate Modern museum.
answers_sanitized: Christo Vladimirov Javacheff AND Jeanne-Claude Denat de Guillebon [prompt on partial answers]

bonuses.leadin: Answer some questions about photographer Annie Leibovitz. For 10 points each:
parts: Annie Leibovitz once mounted one of the gargoyles on the 61st floor of the Chrysler Building in an emulation of this Life magazine photographer.
Annie Leibovitz photographed this artist in Central Park wrapped and tied up -- a reference to his lifelong obsession of wrapping things in fabric with his wife Jeanne-Claude.
Leibovitz is best known for taking photos of Yoko Ono and this Beatle on the day he was shot.
answers_sanitized: Christo Vladimirov Javacheff [accept either underlined part]

bonuses.leadin: A photograph by Annie Leibovitz taken in Central Park shows this man wrapped in fabric. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this mononymous artist who worked with his wife Jeanne-Claude on large-scale environmental artworks, such as Wrapped Coast, Wrapped Reichstag, and Running Fence.
In 1980, a year before doing Christo’s portrait, Leibovitz had photographed this man lying nude next to his wife Yoko Ono on the day that he was assassinated.

In a 2012 interview, Leibovitz noted, “I don’t know if I believe in [this concept] any more.” This concept titles a photo book that includes an image of a man riding a bicycle past a winding stairwell, titled Hyères, France [“yair france”].
answers_sanitized: Christo Vladimirov Javacheff [accept either underlined portion]

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about when art kills, for 10 points each.
parts: During an installation of an Alexander Calder piece at Princeton, a snapped cable resulted in the death of two workmen. Calder originated this type of sculpture whose shapes move in response to touch or air currents.

This artist and his wife once took seven years to set up thousands of yellow umbrellas in California and blue umbrellas in Japan as part of an art project. High winds blew an umbrella in California loose, killing a bystander.

A piece by this sculptor, noted for working with large-scale assemblies of sheet metal, had a part break free from its support and kill a rigger in Minneapolis. He installed Tilted Arc, an arc of rusted steel in New York City's Federal Plaza.
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Answer the following about when art kills, for 10 points each.
parts_sanitized: During an installation of an Alexander Calder piece at Princeton, a snapped cable resulted in the death of two workmen. Calder originated this type of sculpture whose shapes move in response to touch or air currents.

This artist and his wife once took seven years to set up thousands of yellow umbrellas in California and blue umbrellas in Japan as part of an art project. High winds blew an umbrella in California loose, killing a bystander.

A piece by this sculptor, noted for working with large-scale assemblies of sheet metal, had a part break free from its support and kill a rigger in Minneapolis. He installed Tilted Arc, an arc of rusted steel in New York City's Federal Plaza.

bonuses.leadin: A series of yellow umbrellas in this state complemented a group of blue umbrellas half-way around the world in Asia. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this state where one can find Running Fence and the Winchester Mystery House.
Those umbrellas and Running Fence were artworks designed by Jean-Claude and this artist, who also wrapped a bunch of islands in nylon pink.
This other work of Christo consists of unfurled rolls of saffron colored fabric hanging from the namesake structure in Central Park. It was only on public display in 2005.

bonuses.leadin: The artist behind this work also created A Nonsite, Franklin, New Jersey, and it was constructed using materials like basalt rocks. For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this work of Robert Smithson completed in 1970 that was submerged for many years after its creation.
Smithson's oeuvre shares motifs with public-art projects like The Gates and Running Fence created by this man and his wife, Jeanne-Claude.
Christo and Jeanne-Claude once wrapped this building in polypropylene. A famous photo by Yevgeny Khaldei sees a Soviet soldier holding a flag on top of this building.

bonuses.leadin: One work by this artist memorializes the Kent State shootings. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this creator of the Partially Buried Woodshed whose Spiral Jetty can still be seen at Rozel Point at the Great Salt Lake.
This artist and his partner Jeanne-Claude made many pieces of earth-art by wrapping large structures like bridges and islands up in fabric. He also set up “The Gates” in Central Park.
One of the structures Christo and Jeanne-Claude wrapped up was this Berlin building in 1995.
answers: the Reichstag [or Reichstagsgebäude; prompt on answers which only mention the German parliament]

the Reichstag [or Reichstagsgebaude; prompt on answers which only mention the German parliament]
